archive_sweep.notes: Archive command is extension/skill-document based rather than a specify CLI subcommand in this Codex surface. Static sweep found only the current DOC-008 target; TACD-001 was archived on main, so no DOC-008 cleanup was applied.

prerequisites.required_codex_agents: installed in user-scoped Codex agents directory

optional_codex_agents.uat-runbook-author: bundled but not installed as a spawnable Codex role; UAT authoring is fail-open if unavailable

prerequisites.mcp_servers: Some MCP servers not configured; agents will use built-in fallbacks.
prerequisites.model_check: Parent config uses gpt-5.5 with model_reasoning_effort=xhigh; required SpecKit Pro subagents are installed with gpt-5.5/xhigh.

validation_baseline.dependency_install: pnpm --dir docs-site install required approved network access after sandbox DNS ENOTFOUND

plan.step: Archive Sweep: previously merged specs dry-run/apply eligibility

plan.summary: Created spec.md with 3 user stories, 17 functional requirements, 9 acceptance scenarios, and 0 clarification markers.

plan.step: Phase 2: Clarify - IA and Route Boundaries
plan.summary: Answered 4 IA/route questions. Route/sidebar already resolved; consensus clarified that DOC-008 should link from hand-authored reference pages unless it explicitly changes the reference generator.

plan.step: Phase 2: Clarify - IA and Route Boundaries Consensus
plan.summary: Codebase and spec-context analysts agreed with high confidence that generated DOC-007 reference subpages should not receive hand-edited DOC-008 backlinks.
Answered 5 troubleshooting matrix questions. Security-tagged consensus and user confirmation made row categories explicit and restricted inspect command/file cells to read-only inspection guidance.
Codebase, spec-context, and domain-research analysts agreed with high confidence on explicit troubleshooting categories and read-only inspection boundaries; user accepted security-tagged edits.

plan.step: Phase 2: Clarify - Security, Trust, and Sources
plan.summary: Answered 5 security/trust/source-policy questions. Security-tagged consensus and user confirmation expanded the vendor citation inventory, tightened claim-specific evidence policy, and clarified cache/recovery boundaries.

plan.step: Phase 2: Clarify - Security, Trust, and Sources Consensus
plan.summary: Codebase, spec-context, and domain-research analysts agreed with high confidence on the expanded citation set and cache wording; user accepted security-tagged edits.

plan.step: Phase 2: Clarify - Update and Rollback Recovery
plan.summary: Answered 5 update/rollback recovery questions. Recovery cases now require checkpoint/action/side-effect/reload-source structure, and Codex installed state must use CLI JSON/runtime-observation wording rather than stable hardcoded cache-path claims.

plan.step: Phase 2: Clarify - Update and Rollback Recovery Consensus
plan.summary: Codebase, spec-context, and domain-research analysts agreed that hardcoded Codex cache paths are repository/plugin-owned local runtime evidence unless current OpenAI docs document the exact path.
